en.extract:
The Biak scops owl (Otus beccarii) is a species of owl endemic to the twin islands of Biak-Supiori in Cenderawasih Bay, Papua, Indonesia. 
Biak scops owls are 20–25 cm in length. They have dark brown and rufous forms (likely morphs) with long ear-tufts and a pale whitish-brown facial disc. Their call is described as hoarse and corvid-like. Their diet mostly consists of small vertebrates or insects. Primarily lives at forest edges and or within forests; may occasionally live near villages.  They are rarely seen, and little is known about their reproduction.


References

BirdLife Species Factsheet - Biak Scops-owl
Owl pages

The '''Biak scops owl''' ('''''Otus beccarii''''') is a species of [[owl]] endemic to the twin [[island]]s of [[Biak]]-[[Supiori Island|Supiori]] in [[Cenderawasih Bay]], [[Papua (Indonesian province)|Papua]], [[Indonesia]]. 

Biak scops owls are 20–25 cm in length.<ref name=":0">Pratt, T. K., and B. M. Beehler (2015) Birds of New Guinea. Second edition. Princeton University Press, Princeton, NJ, USA.</ref> They have dark brown and rufous forms (likely [[Polymorphism (biology)|morphs]]) with long ear-tufts and a pale whitish-brown facial disc.<ref name=":1">Holt, D. W., R. Berkley, C. Deppe, P. L. Enríquez, J. L. Petersen, J. L. Rangel Salazar, K. P. Segars, K. L. Wood, E. de Juana, C. J. Sharpe, and J. S. Marks (2020). Biak Scops-Owl (''Otus beccarii''), version 1.0. In Birds of the World (J. del Hoyo, A. Elliott, J. Sargatal, D. A. Christie, and E. de Juana, Editors). Cornell Lab of Ornithology, Ithaca, NY, USA. https://doi.org/10.2173/bow.biasco1.01</ref> Their [https://xeno-canto.org/155591 call] is described as hoarse and [[corvid]]-like.<ref name=":1" /> Their diet mostly consists of small vertebrates or insects. Primarily lives at forest edges and or within forests; may occasionally live near villages.<ref name=":0" />  They are rarely seen, and little is known about their reproduction.
